title: Publications | Ministry of Health NZ
content:
## Publications Ngā whakaputanga kōrero
- Assessment and management of people at risk of suicide Publication date: 1 May 2003 These guidelines are intended as a resource for clinical staff in emergency departments and mental health clinicians when assessing and working with people who have made a suicide attempt, or are at risk of taking their own lives. Publication Suicide

## Assessment and management of people at risk of suicide
These guidelines are intended as a resource for clinical staff in emergency departments and mental health clinicians when assessing and working with people who have made a suicide attempt, or are at risk of taking their own lives.
- Healthy Eating: Healthy Action. Oranga Kai: Oranga Pumau : A strategic framework Publication date: 2 March 2003 With increasing rates of preventable health conditions related to poor nutrition and sedentary lifestyles, and the rising obesity epidemic, the need to act now is becoming urgent. But getting people to change their lifestyles is not an easy task. Publication Nutrition Physical activity

## Healthy Eating: Healthy Action. Oranga Kai: Oranga Pumau : A strategic framework
With increasing rates of preventable health conditions related to poor nutrition and sedentary lifestyles, and the rising obesity epidemic, the need to act now is becoming urgent. But getting people to change their lifestyles is not an easy task.
- He Korowai Oranga : Māori Health Strategy Publication date: 2 November 2002 This original He Korowai Oranga : Māori Health Strategy set the direction for Māori health development in the health and disability sector. Publication Māori health

## He Korowai Oranga : Māori Health Strategy
This original He Korowai Oranga : Māori Health Strategy set the direction for Māori health development in the health and disability sector.
- Reducing Inequalities in Health Publication date: 2 September 2002 In New Zealand, ethnic identity is an important dimension of health inequalities. Māori health status is demonstrably poorer than other New Zealanders; actions to improve Māori health also recognise Treaty of Waitangi obligations of the Crown. Pacific peoples also have poorer health than Pākehā . In addition, gender and geographical inequalities are important areas for action. Publication Inequalities

## Reducing Inequalities in Health
In New Zealand, ethnic identity is an important dimension of health inequalities. Māori health status is demonstrably poorer than other New Zealanders; actions to improve Māori health also recognise Treaty of Waitangi obligations of the Crown. Pacific peoples also have poorer health than Pākehā . In addition, gender and geographical inequalities are important areas for action.
- Guidelines for the Control of Methicillin-resistant Staphyloccus Aureus in New Zealand Publication date: 1 August 2002 These Guidelines for the Control of Methicillin-resistant Staphyloccus Aureus (MRSA) in New Zealand are for use by individual health and disability care facilities to develop their own MRSA policy. Publication Antimicrobial resistance

## Guidelines for the Control of Methicillin-resistant Staphyloccus Aureus in New Zealand
These Guidelines for the Control of Methicillin-resistant Staphyloccus Aureus (MRSA) in New Zealand are for use by individual health and disability care facilities to develop their own MRSA policy.
- Antimicrobial resistance
- The Primary Health Care Strategy Publication date: 2 February 2001 A strong primary health care system is central to improving the health of New Zealanders and, in particular, tackling inequalities in health. Publication Primary health care

## The Primary Health Care Strategy
A strong primary health care system is central to improving the health of New Zealanders and, in particular, tackling inequalities in health.
- Involving Families Publication date: 16 November 2000 Guidance for involving families and whanau of mental health consumers/tangata whai ora in care, assessment and treatment processes Publication Mental health
Guidance for involving families and whanau of mental health consumers/tangata whai ora in care, assessment and treatment processes
- Guidelines for the Safe Piercing of Skin Publication date: 2 October 1998 Body piercing and tattooing are activities that can affect public health when operators use unsafe techniques. There are significant hazards posed by contact with blood and body fluid, such as (the risk of) transmitting blood-borne viral diseases. Publication Diseases & conditions

## Guidelines for the Safe Piercing of Skin
Body piercing and tattooing are activities that can affect public health when operators use unsafe techniques. There are significant hazards posed by contact with blood and body fluid, such as (the risk of) transmitting blood-borne viral diseases.
- Diseases & conditions
- The Social, Cultural and Economic Determinants of Health in New Zealand Publication date: 15 June 1998 This report considers the social, cultural and economic determinants of health and provides advice to the Minister of Health on practical strategies that will improve the health of New Zealanders and reduce socioeconomic inequalities in health Publication Inequalities

## The Social, Cultural and Economic Determinants of Health in New Zealand
This report considers the social, cultural and economic determinants of health and provides advice to the Minister of Health on practical strategies that will improve the health of New Zealanders and reduce socioeconomic inequalities in health
- Nga Kupu Oranga Healthy Messages: A health and safety resource for early childhood services Publication date: 2 January 1998 A resource developed for early childhood services to provide information and advice about common illnesses and how to prevent them; the care of children with ongoing conditions such as asthma and diabetes; the Well Child– Tamariki Ora programme; food and nutrition; staff health; and safety issues likely to occur in any early childhood service. Publication Babies and toddlers (0–2 years)

## Nga Kupu Oranga Healthy Messages: A health and safety resource for early childhood services
A resource developed for early childhood services to provide information and advice about common illnesses and how to prevent them; the care of children with ongoing conditions such as asthma and diabetes; the Well Child– Tamariki Ora programme; food and nutrition; staff health; and safety issues likely to occur in any early childhood service.
- Babies and toddlers (0–2 years)
